attendance.record: The daily attendance record - a person's status for a day (Present, Absent, Half-day, On-leave). View sees records, Mark sets a day's status, Edit changes a saved record, Delete removes one. Self scope means your own days only; All means everyone.
attendance.selfPunch: The member's own check-in / check-out clock. Punching needs this grant AND the workspace 'Self check-in' policy turned on (Workspace settings, Attendance). The permission alone is not enough.
attendance.analytics: Org-wide attendance dashboards and reports - overview, muster grid, overtime, compliance, absence patterns. Read-only and workspace-wide.
attendance.export: Download attendance and statutory exports (registers, PF / ESI files).
attendance.events: The raw punch-event log - every check-in and check-out, including from biometric devices. Delete voids an incorrect punch. This is the audit view of punches, not the act of punching (that is Self check-in).
attendance.anomaly: The anomalies queue - flagged irregular punches and patterns, plus the rules that detect them.
attendance.device: Biometric devices and kiosk setup - register, assign and configure punch hardware.
attendance.policy: Attendance policies - late-arrival, early-departure, overtime and comp-off rules applied when attendance is computed.

leave.request: Leave applications - apply for leave, view your requests, and cancel or withdraw them. Self means your own; All means apply or view on anyone's behalf.
leave.approval: Approve or reject leave requests in your approval chain. A person can never approve their own request.
leave.balance: Leave balances per leave type - opening, accrued, used and available.
leave.compOff: Comp-off - claim a worked holiday or weekly-off (apply) and approve claims (decide). A self-claim also needs the workspace self-service policy.
leave.type: Configure the leave-type catalogue - paid and unpaid types, accrual, and year-end carry-forward.
leave.settings: Workspace leave settings - the approval chain, sandwich-leave rule, retro window, and manual balance adjustments.
leave.delegation: Delegate your approval authority to another approver for a coverage window.

regularization.request: Attendance-correction requests - raise a correction for a past day, view your requests, and cancel them. A self-raise also needs the workspace self-service policy.
regularization.approval: Approve or reject attendance-correction requests in your chain. A person can never approve their own.
regularization.settings: Regularization workflow config - approval levels, the retro window, and attachment limits.

holidays.calendar: The workspace holiday calendar - shared, non-working days for everyone. View lets a member see the calendar; Create, Edit and Delete are admin actions. Holidays are workspace-global reference data, so there is no self or all scope - everyone sees the same list.

finance.invoice: Sales tax invoices. View sees invoices, Create and Edit work on drafts, Post finalises a draft into a numbered, ledger-affecting invoice, Delete voids a draft, and Send delivers it by email, WhatsApp or SMS. Self scope means only invoices the member created; All means every invoice. Send and Delete are sensitive: sending spends communication credits and a void removes a billing document.
finance.creditNote: Credit notes against a posted invoice. Creating one reverses output tax and the invoice outstanding (Sec 34), so it is sensitive and ledger-affecting. Self means against the member's own invoices; All means any invoice.
finance.expense: Business expenses (rent, electricity, maintenance, misc). View sees expenses, Create records a new one. Self means only the member's own entries; All means everyone's.
finance.payment: Record a payment received against an invoice. Self means against the member's own invoices; All means any invoice.
finance.report: Finance dashboards and party / profit-and-loss reports. Read-only and workspace-wide, so there is no self or all scope.
finance.gst: GST configuration - rates, returns, and e-invoice / e-way settings. A sensitive compliance surface.
finance.settings: Finance and firm settings - branding, invoice numbering series, and the per-firm compliance profile. A sensitive admin surface.

accrual.title: Accrual
accrual.body: How this leave type is credited to a balance - granted upfront once a year, accrued steadily through the year at a set rate, or not accrued at all (credited manually or via comp-off).

statutory.title: Statutory basis
statutory.body: The labour law this leave type maps to. It drives the advisory minimum-entitlement check - for example the Factories Act or the Shops & Establishments Act. "Voluntary" means the type is company policy with no statutory floor.

yearEnd.title: Year-end handling
yearEnd.body: What happens to an unused balance when the leave year closes - carry a capped number of days into the next year, let the excess lapse, or pay it out as encashment.

approvals.title: Leave Approvals
approvals.subtitle: Review and decide leave and comp-off requests across the workspace.

approvals.emptySub: Nothing matches this filter right now.

lwp.title: LWP - Leave Without Pay
lwp.body: Days in the request that are not covered by a paid balance. They are still approved as leave but are unpaid, so payroll deducts them for that period.

retroactive.title: Retroactive request
retroactive.body: The request covers dates that have already passed. It is allowed within the back-dating window set in Leave Settings, and is flagged here so an approver can give it a closer look.

chain.title: Approval chain
chain.body: The ordered list of approvers this request passes through. Each level must approve before it moves to the next; a rejection at any level stops the request. "Auto-approved" means no approver chain is configured.

settings.title: Leave Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure the approval workflow and manage approver delegations.

accessDenied.title: Access Denied

settings.workflowDesc: Who reviews leave and comp-off requests, and the rules applied when one is raised.
settings.approverChainHint: Requests route through these approvers in order. An empty chain auto-approves every request.
settings.noApprovers: No approvers - requests are auto-approved on submission.
settings.sandwichHint: When on, holidays and weekly-offs that fall inside a leave span are also charged.
settings.retroHint: How far back a past-dated leave request may reach.
settings.maxAttachHint: The most files a member can attach to one request.
settings.delegDesc: An approver can hand their approval authority to a delegate for a coverage window - e.g. while they are away.
settings.revokeConfirmDesc: The delegate immediately loses the ability to act on your behalf.
settings.createHint: You are delegating your own approval authority. The delegate can action requests where you are the approver during the window.

sandwich.title: Sandwich leave
sandwich.body: When on, a holiday or weekly off that falls between two leave days is counted as leave too. With it off, only the actual leave days are deducted from the balance.

delegation.title: Approver delegation
delegation.body: Temporarily hand an approver’s leave-approval duties to another user for a fixed window - useful when an approver is themselves on leave. The delegation lapses automatically when the window ends.

balances.title: Leave Balances
balances.subtitle: Every member's accrued, used, and available leave for the year - with manual corrections.

accessDenied.title: Access Denied
accessDenied.message: You don't have permission to view leave balances. Contact your workspace owner to request access.

balances.emptySub: Balances appear once leave accrues. Check back after the next accrual run.
balances.qtyHint: A positive value credits days; a negative value debits. Half-days allowed.
balances.reasonPlaceholder: Why this correction is being made - recorded on the ledger
balances.reasonRequired: A reason is required for every adjustment.

opening.title: Opening balance
opening.body: The balance carried into this leave year from the previous one - capped by each leave type's year-end carry-forward rule.

credited.title: Credited
credited.body: Days added to the balance during this year - through accrual runs, an upfront annual grant, or approved comp-off.

pending.title: Pending
pending.body: Days locked by leave requests that are submitted but not yet approved. They are held aside so the available figure is not over-promised.

adjustment.title: Manual adjustment
adjustment.body: A correction posted directly to the ledger. A positive value credits days, a negative value debits them. Every adjustment is recorded with a reason for audit.

calendar.title: Who's on Leave
calendar.subtitle: A month view of approved leave across the team, with holidays overlaid.
calendar.loadError: Could not load the leave calendar.

calendar.emptySub: No approved leave falls in the selected month.

legend.title: Reading the calendar
legend.body: Each coloured square marks a day of approved leave, tinted by leave type. A faded square is a half day; a solid one is a full day. Shaded columns are holidays or weekly-offs. Leave days here include both paid days and unpaid days (LWP) - days not covered by a paid balance, which payroll deducts.

lwp.title: LWP - Leave Without Pay
lwp.body: Days in a request that are not covered by a paid balance. They are still approved as leave but are unpaid, so payroll deducts them for that period.

halfDay.title: Half day
halfDay.body: Take leave for only part of a day. "First half" covers the morning, "second half" the afternoon - each counts as 0.5 of a day against the balance.

compOff.title: Compensatory off (comp-off)
compOff.intro: A paid day off an employee earns by working on a day they were not scheduled - a public holiday or a weekly off - instead of being paid extra for it.
compOff.step1: The employee works on a holiday or weekly off.
compOff.step2: They claim the compensatory off here, choosing the date they worked.
compOff.step3: A manager reviews and approves the claim.
compOff.step4: Approved days are banked as a dated "lot" of compensatory off.
compOff.step5: To take the day off, the employee applies in My Leave with the "Comp-off" type - unused days expire when the lot does.

lot.title: Compensatory off lot
lot.body: Each approved claim becomes its own lot - a parcel of compensatory off days with a fixed expiry. Lots are used oldest-first, so days closest to lapsing are spent before newer ones.

expiry.title: Lot expiry
expiry.body: Compensatory off does not last forever - every lot lapses a set number of days after it is earned. Days you do not use before a lot's expiry are forfeited.

bands.title: Bradford bands
bands.tooltipBody1: Bradford Factor = spells² × absent days. Bands are advisory thresholds used in UK-origin HR practice.
bands.tooltipBody2: These bands never trigger automatic actions in ManekHR. Use them as a prompt for manager conversations, not as disciplinary triggers.

table.title: Patterns to watch
table.colSpellsTooltip: A spell is a contiguous block of absence days. For example, Mon–Wed absent = 1 spell of 3 days.
table.colBradfordTooltipBody1: Bradford Factor = spells² × absent days. It penalises frequent short absences more than long ones, on the theory that unpredictable absence disrupts operations more.
table.colBradfordTooltipBody2: Originally a UK HR methodology, Bradford is used as an advisory signal only in ManekHR. It does not change pay, leave balance, or attendance status.
table.colBradfordTooltipBody3: Industry bands: Low < 50, Moderate 50–124, High 125–399, Very High 400+.

regularizationsList.headerExplainerBody: Regularization is the Indian-payroll term for an after-the-fact correction to an attendance record - typically when a worker forgot to punch in/out or a biometric device missed the punch. Workers raise a request; the configured approver chain decides.

regularizationSettings.title: Regularization Settings

tooltips.approvalLevels: How many managers must approve a correction request before it takes effect. 1 means only the direct manager approves. 2 or 3 adds additional levels up the reporting chain.
tooltips.maxDaysBack: Employees can only raise a correction request for attendance within this many days in the past. For example, 30 means they can correct attendance for the last 30 days only.
tooltips.fallbackApprover: When an employee's reporting manager is not set or the approval chain cannot be resolved, this person steps in as the approver. Recommended: set this to an HR admin.
tooltips.maxAttachments: How many supporting files (photos, documents) an employee can attach to a correction request. Set to 0 to disable file uploads for requests.

overview.kpiOutstandingAdvancesInfo: An advance is a small, interest-free amount paid to a member before payday. It is taken back over a few months by reducing future salary. This total shows how much is still to be recovered across all active advance plans.

overview.kpiActiveLoansInfo: A loan is a larger amount that may carry interest and a fixed EMI plan. Unlike an advance, a loan can span many months and may have a small taxable perquisite. This total shows the outstanding principal across all active and paused loans.
overview.kpiBonusCommissionInfo: These are extra pay items added on top of salary this month. They are not taken back later. This total includes commission added from a payment as well as any directly recorded bonus, commission, or incentive. No double-counting.

bonus.pageSubtitle: Statutory bonus (Payment of Bonus Act) and festival bonus for your team
bonus.loadError: Could not load bonus data. Please try again.
bonus.infoTooltip: Statutory Bonus is required by law (Payment of Bonus Act / Code on Wages). It applies to employees earning up to Rs 21,000/month. The minimum payout is 8.33% and the maximum is 20% of the calculation wage (Rs 7,000 or applicable minimum wage, whichever is higher). It must be paid by 30 November for an April-March financial year. Festival/Discretionary Bonus is entirely your choice - no legal minimum or maximum. Both types are fully taxable (TDS applies) and are excluded from PF wages. All bonus amounts go into one ledger - no double count.
bonus.singleLedgerDesc: Statutory bonus, festival bonus, and any bonus added directly from the adjustment form all write to the same ledger. Totals shown here include every entry point exactly once.
bonus.statStatutoryHint: Total statutory bonus paid this financial year
bonus.statDiscretionaryHint: Total festival or discretionary bonus paid this financial year
bonus.statTotalHint: All bonus types combined, single ledger
bonus.statRunsHint: Number of bonus disbursement runs this financial year
bonus.eligibilityDesc: Applicable to employees earning up to Rs 21,000/month. Calculation base is Rs 7,000 or the applicable minimum wage (whichever is higher). At least 30 working days required. Minimum 8.33%, maximum 20%. Payable by 30 November for an April-March financial year. Excluded from PF wages. TDS applies.
bonus.percentTooltip: Enter the applicable bonus percentage between 8.33% (legal minimum) and 20% (legal maximum). This is your allocable surplus percent. Confirm the correct value with your CA before running.
bonus.previewHint: Select the financial year and percentage above, then click Preview to see per-member computed amounts before committing.
bonus.previewError: Could not compute preview. Check inputs and try again.

bonus.runIdempotentDesc: Members already paid in a previous run for this financial year are skipped automatically. Running twice does not double-pay anyone.
bonus.lastWageTooltip: The most recent base salary for this member in the financial year. Eligibility is checked against Rs 21,000 ceiling.
bonus.calcWageTooltip: The wage used for computing the bonus amount. This is Rs 7,000 or the applicable minimum wage, whichever is higher - NOT the actual salary.
bonus.festivalCreditTooltip: If a festival bonus with countsAsStatutory=true exists for this member in the same FY, only the shortfall above that amount is posted as statutory.
bonus.emptyPreview: No members found for this financial year. Try a different year.
bonus.festivalInfoDesc: A festival or discretionary bonus is entirely your decision - no statutory minimum or maximum applies. It is fully taxable (TDS applies) and excluded from PF. ESI treatment for festival bonus is case-law-dependent; confirm with your compliance team before disbursal.
bonus.festivalEsiDesc: ESI treatment for festival/discretionary bonus is disputed in case law. The system marks it as taxable but does not enforce an ESI answer. Please confirm the correct ESI treatment with your compliance team.
bonus.countsAsStatutoryLabel: Also count this as the statutory bonus for this financial year
bonus.countsAsStatutoryTooltip: When checked: this festival bonus will satisfy the statutory obligation for each covered member (up to the computed statutory amount). If you later run the statutory bonus for the same financial year, members already covered by this festival bonus will be skipped or will only receive the shortfall. This prevents double-payment of the statutory obligation.
bonus.countsAsStatutoryNote: The statutory run for this financial year will credit this festival bonus toward each member's statutory obligation and post only the shortfall (if any).

bonus.configCaDesc: Statutory bonus thresholds are set by law (Payment of Bonus Act / Code on Wages). The defaults shown are the current statutory values. Change these only after confirming the applicable amounts with your Chartered Accountant.
bonus.configEligibilityCeilingTooltip: Employees earning more than this amount per month are not eligible for statutory bonus. The statutory default is Rs 21,000/month.
bonus.configCalcFloorTooltip: The wage used to compute the bonus amount. Statutory default is Rs 7,000/month. Override to the applicable minimum wage for your state/category if it is higher than Rs 7,000.
bonus.configAllocableSurplusTooltip: Your CA-certified allocable surplus percentage for the year. This determines the applicable bonus percent (clamped between 8.33% and 20%). Set to 0 to always use the statutory minimum of 8.33%.

erp.title: Team, attendance, salary, and roles, in one ERP.
erp.sub: ManekHR ERP manages your staff records, attendance, and salary, with the right access for every role. Built for diamond-polishing units in Surat.
erp.trialNote: Full-access free trial. No card needed.

free.a: Yes, to start. Staff records, attendance, and salary tracking are free to try, with no card needed. Paid plans add more as your team grows.

setup.q: How long does it take to set up?
setup.a: A few minutes. Sign up with your phone number, add your unit, and start adding staff. There is no lengthy onboarding or training needed.

pricing.a: ManekHR is a simple monthly subscription based on your team size. There is no commission on anything and no hidden charges.

attendance.a: Attendance can be marked from a phone, a shared kiosk, or a biometric device. Supervisors can mark it for their whole team in one place.

payroll.a: Salary is calculated automatically from attendance, whether your staff are on daily-wage, monthly, or piece-rate pay. Advances and deductions are handled in the same place.

languages.q: Which languages does it work in?
languages.a: English, Gujarati, and Hindi or Gujarati written in English letters. You pick your language once and can switch anytime.

compliance.a: Yes. Payroll supports statutory deductions like PF, ESI, PT, and TDS, so your payslips stay compliant.

roles.q: Can I control who sees what?
roles.a: Yes. ManekHR uses role-based access, so owners, accountants, and supervisors each see only the information and actions relevant to their role.

data.a: Yes. Your data stays yours, we never sell it, and every change to attendance or salary is logged in a clear audit trail.

support.a: Our team is reachable by email and WhatsApp, and we help you get your unit set up correctly from day one.

switch.q: Can I switch from paper or spreadsheets?
switch.a: Yes. Most units start by adding their current staff list and attendance records, and are fully set up within a day.

trial.q: How does the free trial work?
trial.a: New ERP workspaces start with a full-access free trial. Every feature unlocked, no credit card. When it ends, you simply pick a plan and your data stays with you. The trial length is shown above and on the plan cards.

whichPlan.q: Which plan is right for my team?
whichPlan.a: Free suits up to 5 people getting organised. Starter adds leave and full attendance, Growth adds full payroll and statutory compliance, and Business adds machines, production, multi-location, and advanced reports. Use the team-size helper above for a recommendation.

billing.a: Paid plans are a one-year term. Pay the year upfront (with any upfront discount shown) or in 12 interest-free monthly installments. Plans auto-renew yearly and we email you before each renewal.

gst.a: Each plan card shows whether GST is included or added on top, and at what rate. Your invoices are GST-ready, so you can claim input credit.

switch.q: Can I upgrade or downgrade later?
switch.a: Yes. Change plans anytime as your team grows or your needs change. Your data and history carry over.

cancel.a: You can cancel anytime with no lock-in. You keep access until the end of your paid term, and your data remains available per our retention policy.

grDetail.cancelled: GRN Return cancelled - stock has been restored

stock.title: Stock Summary
stock.emptyBody: Add items in the Items master and record an opening stock or purchase bill to see your inventory here.
stock.reorderTip: Reorder level is the on-hand quantity at which an item should be re-purchased. Items below this level are flagged so you can raise a purchase in time.
stock.valuationTip: Stock value is computed from the average cost of each item on hand (weighted-average method). Use it to gauge working capital tied up in inventory.

godowns.title: Godowns
godowns.tip: A godown (warehouse) is a physical location where stock is held. Split stock across godowns to track it by floor, branch, or warehouse.
godowns.emptyBody: Main Godown is being created for your firm. This takes a moment.
godowns.defaultHint: You're using the default godown. Add more godowns to split stock by location, floor, or warehouse.

batches.title: Batches
batches.tip: A batch groups units produced together (often with a manufacturing and expiry date) so stock can be tracked and recalled by batch.
batches.emptyBody: Batch registry will be available once manufacturing vouchers are enabled.

serials.title: Serials
serials.tip: A serial number identifies a single physical unit so it can be tracked individually from purchase to sale, return, or scrap.
serials.emptyBody: Serial numbers are registered when you post a purchase for items with serial tracking enabled.
serials.scrapConfirmDesc: The unit will be removed from stock and cannot be recovered.

transfers.title: Stock Transfers
transfers.emptyBody: Use stock transfers to move goods between your godowns.

wastage.title: Wastage Register
wastage.tip: Wastage records goods damaged, expired, or lost. Each entry is double-entry posted to your books so the loss is reflected in your accounts.
wastage.emptyBody: Record goods damaged, expired, or lost during processing. All wastage is double-entry posted to your books.

lots.title: Lots
lots.tip: A lot is a tracked batch of stock (with optional inward and expiry dates) used for traceability and first-expiry-first-out picking.
lots.emptyBody: Lots are created automatically when you post a purchase bill for items with lot tracking enabled. You can also create lots manually.

samples.title: Samples & Consignments
samples.tip: Samples and consignments are goods sent to a customer for approval. Accepted goods convert to a tax invoice; the rest are returned to stock.
samples.emptyBody: Track goods sent to customers for approval or held on consignment. Accepted goods auto-convert to a tax invoice.

labels.title: Print Labels
labels.generateFailed: Label generation failed. Verify the item has a valid item code and try again.

transfer.godownsDiffer: From and To godowns must differ
transfer.godownsDifferAlert: From and To godowns must be different
transfer.postConfirmDesc: Once posted, this voucher cannot be edited.

wastage.postConfirmDesc: Once posted, this voucher cannot be edited.

sample.postConfirmDesc: Once posted, this voucher cannot be edited.

bom.title: Bill of Materials
bom.tip: A Bill of Materials (BoM) lists the components and quantities needed to produce one batch of a finished item. It drives material consumption and costing on a manufacturing voucher.

vouchers.tip: A manufacturing voucher records a production run: it consumes input materials (per the BoM) and creates the finished item, posting the cost difference as variance.

listCommon.tip: Job-work is sending material to (or receiving it from) a principal for processing under GST. Custody of the goods stays with the principal; ITC-04 reports the movement.

outward.title: Outward Challans
outward.emptyBody: Return material to a principal after completing work. Select a principal to see their pending lots.
outward.noMatch: No challans on this page match "{query}"

inward.title: Inward Challans
inward.emptyBody: Record material received from a principal to begin tracking job-work. Each challan creates custody lots for the principal's material.
inward.cancelConfirmDesc: Lots have not been created yet.
inward.noMatch: No challans on this page match "{query}"

invoices.title: JW Invoices
invoices.emptyBody: JW Invoices are automatically created when you post an Outward Challan. Post an Outward Challan to get started.
invoices.noMatch: No invoices on this page match "{query}"

itc04.title: ITC-04 Quarterly Report
itc04.tip: ITC-04 is the quarterly GST return that reports goods sent to and received from a job-worker. File it on the GST portal using the exported JSON.
itc04.exportTooltip: Downloads a GSTN-spec ITC-04 JSON file for the selected quarter. Share with your CA for portal filing.
itc04.footerNote: hash and signature fields are empty - computed by GSTN portal on upload
itc04.exportFailed: Failed to generate ITC-04 JSON. Ensure all challans have valid party GSTIN.

pending.title: Pending Material Register
pending.tip: Pending material is principal-owned stock still in your custody. Track it so each lot is returned (or treated as a deemed supply) within the GST time limit.
pending.emptyBody: All principal-owned material has been returned. Post Inward Challans to begin tracking new job-work custody.

master.title: Items
master.hsnSacTip: HSN (for goods) or SAC (for services) is the GST classification code that sets the applicable tax rate. Enter the 4-8 digit code from your rate notification.
master.trackBatchTip: Turn on to track this item by batch or lot (with inward/expiry dates). Use it for items you recall or pick first-expiry-first-out.

ebitda.info: EBITDA is earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortisation. It shows core operating profit before financing and accounting effects.

payments.title: Payment Receipts
payments.info: A payment receipt records money received from a party and how it is applied against their outstanding invoices.
payments.emptyText: No payment receipts yet. Click "New Receipt" to get started.
payments.emptyBody: Record money received from a party by creating your first receipt.

bankAccounts.title: Bank Accounts
bankAccounts.info: Bank accounts hold your firm's balances and feed bank reconciliation. Mark one as default for new receipts and payments.
bankAccounts.empty: No bank accounts yet. Create one to get started.
bankAccounts.deleteConfirmBody: This will soft-delete the account. You cannot delete an account with a non-zero balance.
bankAccounts.deleteConfirmBodyDetail: This action cannot be undone. Accounts with non-zero balance cannot be deleted.

reconcile.title: Bank Reconciliation
reconcile.info: Bank reconciliation matches your imported bank statement against ledger entries so every line is accounted for.
reconcile.noFirm: No firm found. Please set up a firm first.
reconcile.mobileWarning: Bank reconciliation is optimised for desktop. Please use a wider screen.
reconcile.emptyBody: Upload your first bank statement to start reconciling. Supports HDFC, ICICI, SBI, Axis, Kotak, and 4 more banks.

cashRegisters.title: Cash Registers
cashRegisters.info: Cash registers track physical cash on hand. Use day-end tally to count and reconcile, and replenish petty cash up to its imprest amount.
cashRegisters.noFirm: Please set up a firm first via Finance > Dashboard.
cashRegisters.empty: No cash registers found. Create one via Finance firm setup.

cheques.title: Cheque Register
cheques.info: The cheque register tracks issued and received cheques, including post-dated cheques (PDCs) and their maturity dates.

contras.info: A contra entry moves money between your own cash and bank accounts (for example, a cash deposit into the bank). It does not affect any party.
contras.noFirm: Please create a firm first via Finance > Dashboard.

journalVouchers.title: Journal Vouchers
journalVouchers.info: A journal voucher records an accounting entry directly against ledger accounts with balanced debits and credits, for adjustments that are not a sale, purchase, or payment.
journalVouchers.noFirm: Please set up a firm first via Finance > Dashboard.

loans.title: Loans
loans.info: Loans track borrowings such as term loans, overdrafts, and cash credit, with EMI scheduling and an amortisation schedule.
loans.noFirm: No firm found. Set up a firm in Finance first.
loans.emiSkipped: EMI already posted for this month.
loans.closeConfirmBody: Mark this loan as fully repaid. This action cannot be undone.
loans.historyHint: EMI history is visible in the Schedule tab - filter by Status = Paid to see posted entries.

list.portalTooltip: Share statement and invoices with this party - no login required.

index.title: Reminder Engine
index.subtitle: Automate payment follow-ups via in-app, email, SMS, push, and WhatsApp channels.
index.settingsDesc: Configure reminder channels, dispatch time, and global opt-out settings.
index.rulesDesc: Create and manage reminder rules that trigger on invoice due dates or overdue events.
index.logsDesc: View the audit trail of all reminders dispatched - sent, failed, or skipped.
index.templatesDesc: Default templates are loaded automatically. Template customisation coming in a future release.

logs.title: Reminder Logs
logs.subtitle: Audit trail of all reminder dispatch attempts. Recipients are pre-masked by the API.
logs.empty: No reminder logs found for the selected filters.

rules.title: Reminder Rules
rules.subtitle: Rules define when and how reminders are dispatched to parties.
rules.deleteDescription: This cannot be undone. Any queued reminders using this rule will be skipped.
rules.empty: No reminder rules yet. Click "Create Rule" to add one.

ruleNew.title: Create Reminder Rule
ruleNew.subtitle: Define when and how a reminder is dispatched to parties.

ruleEdit.title: Edit Rule: {name}
ruleEdit.subtitle: Update the rule configuration. Changes take effect on the next dispatch run.

ruleForm.daysOffsetExtra: Negative = before due (e.g., -3 means 3 days before due date). Positive = after due (e.g., +7 means 7 days overdue).
ruleForm.cooldownExtra: Minimum hours between sends to the same party for this rule.
ruleForm.partyExtra: Select a specific party or leave as 'Global' to apply to all parties.
ruleForm.priorityExtra: Higher priority rules are evaluated first when multiple rules match the same party and trigger.

settings.title: Reminder Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure global reminder behaviour for this firm. Per-rule overrides take precedence over defaults.
settings.dispatchNote: Note: The actual dispatch cron runs at 07:30 IST daily. This setting is recorded but does not currently change the dispatch schedule.
settings.minimumOutstandingExtra: Only remind parties with outstanding above this threshold.
settings.maxRemindersExtra: Safety cap - prevents accidental spam runs.
settings.smsWarningBody: Requires DLT registration with TRAI (Principal Entity + sender header + template approval). Enable only after registration is complete. Unregistered sends will be blocked by Indian telecom operators.
settings.pushWarningBody: Requires FCM token registration on user devices via the mobile app. Push notifications will silently fail for users without a registered token. Ask users to log in to the mobile app to register their device.
settings.whatsAppWarningBody: Requires an AiSensy account, a Meta-approved WhatsApp message template, and an active Live campaign. Sends will fail until template approval is complete. Contact AiSensy support to initiate the approval process.
settings.creditsTopUp: Top up to keep reminders flowing.

business.pageDescription: Legal identity, address, and accounting preferences for {firm}. These appear on your invoices and drive tax.
business.loadErrorSubtitle: Please refresh the page or try again in a moment.
business.identityHint: Your registered name and tax identifiers. Fetch from GSTIN to auto-fill.
business.addressHint: Printed on invoices. The state drives CGST+SGST vs IGST. Manage extra state registrations under Tax / GSTINs.
business.accountingHint: Defaults for your books, invoices, and stock.
business.fyStartMonthTooltip: Most Indian businesses start their financial year in April (month 4).
business.booksBeginTooltip: The date your accounting records start in manekhr.
business.aatoTooltip: Above Rs 5 crore (500 lakhs) e-invoicing becomes mandatory.
business.roundingPolicyTooltip: Round to Rupee shows whole-rupee invoice totals (common on Indian invoices).
business.inventoryValuationTooltip: How stock value is calculated for cost of goods sold.

regularizationSettings.title: Regularization Settings
regularizationSettings.subtitle: Configure how attendance correction requests work for your workspace.

tooltips.approvalLevels: How many managers must approve a correction request before it takes effect. 1 means only the direct manager approves. 2 or 3 adds additional levels up the reporting chain.
tooltips.maxDaysBack: Employees can only raise a correction request for attendance within this many days in the past. For example, 30 means they can correct attendance for the last 30 days only.
tooltips.fallbackApprover: When an employee's reporting manager is not set or the approval chain cannot be resolved, this person steps in as the approver. Recommended: set this to an HR admin.
tooltips.maxAttachments: How many supporting files (photos, documents) an employee can attach to a correction request. Set to 0 to disable file uploads for requests.

salarySettings.ownerOnlyWarning: Only the workspace owner can change these settings.

disbursement.helperText: When monthly salary can be recorded as paid (e.g. January's pay between 1 and 6 February). Applies to staff you delegate salary to; owners can record a payment any time.
disbursement.salaryDateHint: Day of the following month on which salary can be paid (1–28).
disbursement.payoutWindowHint: Number of days after salary date during which payment is allowed.
disbursement.advanceRequestDayHint: Last day of the current month on which employees may submit advance salary requests.
disbursement.advancePayoutDayHint: Day of month on which approved advances are disbursed (1-28). Leave blank for no fixed day.
disbursement.advancesGroupHint: When employees may request an advance, when it is paid out, and optional limits.
disbursement.advanceRequestWindowHint: Choose when employees may submit advance salary requests each month.
disbursement.eligibilityCapsHint: Optional guardrails checked when an employee requests an advance. Leave a field blank to turn that limit off.
disbursement.maxPercentOfNetHint: A single advance cannot exceed this percent of the employee’s monthly salary.
disbursement.maxPerYearHint: Most advance requests an employee may submit in a calendar year.
disbursement.minTenureHint: Months an employee must have worked (from their join date) before they can request.

salaryLoss.regularizationWindowHint: Days after month-end during which attendance can be regularized before salary-loss is posted (default 45).
salaryLoss.enabledHint: When enabled, unpaid-leave days post a debit entry to the salary COA ledger after the regularization window closes.

attendanceRules.holidayHint: When enabled, declared holidays are counted as present days for salary calculation.
attendanceRules.weekOffHint: When enabled, weekly off days are counted as present days for salary calculation.
attendanceRules.lateMarkHint: When enabled, an employee marked as late deducts 0.5 days from their monthly salary calculation.
